tooltips.baseNube: Cloud Base AGL (km): Estimated height of the cloud base above ground. Calculated from 2m temperature and 2m dew point.
tooltips.cizalladura: Low-Level Wind Shear (LLWS) / Mean wind forecast reliability
tooltips.techo: Altitude (km) above sea level (MSL) of the usable flight ceiling (= boundary layer thickness BLH x 0.85 + ECMWF 9km cell mean ground altitude)
tooltips.tooltipMeteoCompleto:
🌦️ Weather Parameters

🌦️: General weather (cloud cover and precipitation)
💦: Precipitation in mm (liters/m²)
💦?: Probability (%) of precipitation exceeding 0.1 mm (liters/m²)
180 m: Wind at 180 m above ground (km/h)
120 m: Wind at 120 m above ground (km/h)
80 m: Wind at 80 m above ground (km/h)
10 m: Wind at 10 m above ground (km/h)
: Max gusts at 10 m above ground (km/h)
: Wind direction at 10 m above ground
: Low-Level Wind Shear by speed / Reliability of the mean wind forecast
Ceiling: Altitude (km) above sea level (MSL) of the usable flight ceiling (= boundary layer thickness BLH x 0.85 + ECMWF 9km cell mean ground altitude)
CAPE: Convective Available Potential Energy (J/kg)
CIN: Convective Inhibition (J/kg in absolute value)

Note: The essential cloud base data ☁️↓ (CBH = Cloud Base Height) is not available yet.
tooltips.tooltipCondiciones:
⭐ Scores (0-10)

The system automatically calculates two scores for each takeoff based on the selected time range. Both are color-coded with a gradient from red to green.

1. Takeoff Conditions (top row):
Evaluates takeoff viability by analyzing the hourly forecast against the preferences set in ⚙️ Settings:
  • Mean wind limits (minimum, ideal, and maximum).
  • Maximum gust limit.
  • Takeoff orientation(s).
  • Rain (automatic veto and 0 points for the affected hour).
2. Conditions for staying in thermals or starting XC (bottom row):
Evaluates the Takeoff Conditions score and the thermal potential for long-distance flights (Cross Country) using data from the ECMWF model:
  • Ceiling: Rewards high ceilings above the average relief height and penalizes low ones (🟩 ≥ 1500m | 🟥 ≤ 800m).
  • CAPE: Rewards blue days or harmless cumulus, and penalizes extreme values due to overdevelopment or storm risk (🟩 0-400 | 🟧 400-800 | 🟥 > 800 J/kg).
  • CIN: Penalizes high convective inhibition (inversion) that acts as a cap, hindering thermal formation (🟩 ≤ 50 | 🟥 > 150 J/kg).
Note: The XC score is independent of takeoff orientation or wind. It purely evaluates the thermal potential of the air mass in that area. It is only zeroed (0 points) if rain or severe storms are forecast.

⚠️ Warning: The essential cloud base data (CBH = Cloud Base Height) is missing to know if the takeoff will be cloud-covered. This is an ECMWF value not yet available in the gateway (requested in March 2026, pending with no scheduled date). You must consult other services.

By default, takeoffs are automatically reordered by the first score (Takeoff Conditions), from highest to lowest. You can change this behavior in: ⚙️ Settings > Other options > Sort by XC Conditions.

tabla.techoTooltip:
Usable flight ceiling: {{altitudMSL}} m MSL
Calculation = {{espesorUtil}} m usable thickness AGL ({{pct}}% of the theoretical {{blh}} m BLH) + {{elevacion}} m altitude (ECMWF 9km cell mean ground level)

paso1.descripcion:
This is the screen for regular use of the application.

It shows a table with all the takeoffs you have selected as favorites, along with their forecast and condition scores (for takeoff and for starting XC routes).

Takeoffs are always automatically sorted by their takeoff condition score (from highest to lowest).

selectorHorario.descripcion:
Adjust this slider from both ends to select the time range you are interested in.

The table will only show those hours, and the condition score will be recalculated for that specific time interval.

diasSemana.descripcion:
These day-of-the-week buttons make it easy to select that day's time range. This will be the typical use of the app: a quick look at the 'possible' takeoffs for that day.

👉🏽 I will select this one as an example so you can see how it works.

diaSeleccionado.descripcion:
Now the table only shows that day with the automatically selected time range.

💡 You can move the sliders to your specific time range to get a more accurate score. You can customize this 'automatic' daily time range in ⚙️ Settings.

columnaMeteo.titulo: 🌦️Weather Column
columnaMeteo.descripcion:
Shows weather data.

Click the icon to see the meaning of each parameter.

filaCizalladura.descripcion: This special row is a combined indicator. It is a color-coded traffic light that simultaneously shows two concurrent data points: Low-Level Wind Shear and the Reliability of the 10m wind forecast.

columnaPuntuacion.descripcion:
The system calculates two scores (0 to 10) for each takeoff and for the selected time range: Takeoff Conditions and Conditions for maintaining thermals or starting Cross Country (XC).

Takeoffs are automatically reordered by Takeoff Condition score.

In ⚙️ Settings, you can customize your limits for the calculation.

btnInfo.titulo: Takeoff Information
btnInfo.descripcion: Shows more complete takeoff information and links to its forecast on Windy, Meteo-parapente, and Meteoblue.

btnOrigen.titulo: Origin Point
btnOrigen.descripcion:
Here you choose the origin point for the distance filter.

You can choose the point using a map or your current location.

btnIncNoFavs.titulo: + Include non-favorites
btnIncNoFavs.descripcion:
Allows you to temporarily include all available takeoffs (favorites and non-favorites) in the filter.

💡 Useful for quickly searching for flight conditions in takeoffs from different areas outside your favorites zone.

btnMapa.titulo: Takeoff Map
btnMapa.descripcion:
Shows the takeoff on the map.

💡 The map includes additional info, various filters, heatmaps, and other useful tools to explore.

navHome.titulo: Home
navHome.descripcion:
Shows the table with all your favorite takeoffs. If there are searches or filters active, it clears them.

💡 If you get lost, use this button to return to the "normal" view.

navSearch.titulo: Search
navSearch.descripcion: Use this to search takeoffs by name or administrative area (usually the province). You can type without accents for speed.

navDistance.titulo: Distance
navDistance.descripcion:
Shows a slider to filter takeoffs within a radius around a point.

👉🏽 I will press this button now to open the filter.

filtroDistancia.titulo: Distance
filtroDistancia.descripcion:
This filter lets you see only the takeoffs around a specific point.

💡 Useful for quickly finding takeoffs within a driving range from your location.

sliderDistancia.descripcion:
Drag this slider to choose the maximum distance.

The table will show only takeoffs within that radius.

navMap.titulo: Map
navMap.descripcion: Paragliding takeoff map with extensive information: takeoff search, filters by orientation, number of flights, year of the last flight, average distance, a heatmap with more than 1.3 million exact recorded takeoff points, and much more information.

navSettings.descripcion:
Here you can Edit favorites (to add or remove your usual takeoffs), customize parameters, enable options, and view information about weather data updates.

💡 Each option has an information button for details.

guiaFavoritos.preguntaVerGuia: Would you like to see a visual guide for this screen?
guiaFavoritos.podrasVerla: You can see it anytime using the button on this screen.

intro.descripcion:
This screen shows all available takeoffs. Select the ones you use regularly. The normal app view will only show these favorites.

You could select them all, but depending on your device, it might slow down searches and daily usage.

Currently covers Spain, Portugal, Pyrenees, and major Alpine areas. This is a growing project.

celdaFavorito.titulo: ↔ Favorites
celdaFavorito.descripcion:
Mark or unmark your favorites here.

Changes are saved automatically.

cabeceraFavorito.titulo: ↔ Header Favorites
cabeceraFavorito.descripcion:
Mark or unmark all currently visible takeoffs at once.

💡 Example: Filter for "Huesca" and mark them all. Or filter by distance and mark all within 50 km.

btnInfoDespegue.titulo: Takeoff Information
btnInfoDespegue.descripcion: Shows detailed takeoff info and links to forecasts on other sites.

btnMapaDespegue.titulo: Takeoff Map
btnMapaDespegue.descripcion:
Shows the takeoff on the map.

💡 The map includes additional info about the takeoff, various filters, takeoff heatmaps, and several useful tools worth exploring.

buscador.titulo: Search
buscador.descripcion: Search for takeoffs by typing the region, province, or name. You can type without accents for speed.

filtroDistancia.titulo: Distance
filtroDistancia.descripcion:
This is a filter that allows you to show only takeoffs around a specific point.

💡 It can help you quickly find takeoffs within a distance radius of your choice around a point.

btnOrigen.titulo: Origin point
btnOrigen.descripcion:
With this button, you choose the center of the distance search radius.

You can select the point using a map or your current location.

sliderDistancia.descripcion: By dragging this slider, you choose the maximum distance in kilometers.

btnVerSoloFavs.titulo: ↔ Show Only Favorites
btnVerSoloFavs.descripcion:
Toggle between showing only your favorites or all takeoffs.

💡 Useful for checking your selected favorites at a glance.

btnDesmarcarTodos.titulo: Unmark All Favorites
btnDesmarcarTodos.descripcion: Remove all takeoffs from your favorites list at once.

btnImportar.descripcion: Open a file with favorite takeoffs.

btnExportar.descripcion:
Saves a file with your current favorite takeoffs.

Note: Favorites are actually saved automatically every time you mark one. This button is used to make a copy of all of them in an external file (as a backup or to share it).

💡 If you travel across several distant flight zones, you might want to have the favorites for each zone saved in different files.

btnGuiaEdicion.titulo: Quick Guide

btnFinalizar.descripcion: Exit to the main screen to see the forecast for your selected favorites.

paso1.html:
🪂 Fly Decision
Where to fly?

🌦️ 4-day hourly forecast.

📊 Automated condition scoring for Takeoff and XC.

🗺️ Takeoff map.

paso2.html:
🪂 Fly Decision. Automated weather forecast analysis for paragliding.

🙄 What is it for?: to help you decide where to fly. It shows a table with your favorite takeoffs, their forecasts, and performs an automated analysis of those weather conditions.

🧮 What analysis does it perform?: it compares the forecast against your configured mean wind and max gust limits and each takeoff's orientation; it also analyzes wind at altitudes near the takeoff (80, 120, and 180 m), providing insight into wind gradient and forecast reliability. It shows key XC data (ceiling, CAPE, and CIN).

With all this info, it scores each takeoff from 0 to 10 and color-codes 🟩 🟨 🟥 the data for your chosen time range. You can customize all limits to match your preferences.

paso3.html:
💨️ Weather data: hourly forecast for 4 days (96h). Updated 8 times a day with official Météo-France data from Arome-HD 1.3km (0-48h) and Arpege 7km (48-96h) models, and 4 times a day with ECMWF data (0-96h).

🆓 Cost and privacy: 100% free, no subscriptions, no ads, and no tracking (cookies, telemetry,...). Your settings are saved privately in your browser. It is a free and open project, operational since 2026 and evolving.

paso4.html:
🤔 Is it complicated?

✅ Basic use: select your favorite takeoffs the first time and you will always have the table with their forecasts and automated analysis for each day.

✅ Intermediate use: you can select a time range (days or hours) and see the automatic condition score for that interval. You can filter takeoffs by distance radius from your home or any location.

✅ Advanced use: you can customize in ⚙️ Settings the default limits for mean wind (minimum, ideal, and maximum) and maximum gusts. Your preferences will be used for the analysis, coloring, and scoring of conditions.

paso5.html:
️👉 Keep in mind...

👉 Only favorite takeoffs are shown. You can select them all, but it might slow down the app on some devices.

👉️ Sorting is automatic by score (10 to 0).

👉️ Cloud Base (CBH) data is currently pending from the provider. Always check multiple sources before flying.

👉️ Coverage: Spain, Portugal, Pyrenees, and Alps (French, Swiss, Italian).

paso6.html:
👍 What now?

You need to mark your favorite takeoffs.

crash.errorCarga:
ℹ️ Error loading table.

Possible cause: too many favorites for your device's resources.

Edit Favorites screen will open so you can unmark some and reduce load.

If you think it's temporary: click Finish editing favorites, restart device and retry.
crash.alertFallback: The app is having trouble loading. Favorites editor enabled to reduce takeoff count.

salirApp.pregunta: Do you want to exit the application?

novedadesXC.html:
• Independent XC Score: Evaluates only thermal potential (Ceiling, CAPE, CIN) ignoring takeoff wind. Only scores 0 with rain or storm risk.
  • Ceiling in Altitude (MSL): Now shows altitude above sea level, providing better precision for distance planning.
  • Realistic Ceiling: Forecast is reduced by 15% to compensate for paraglider sink rate.

💡 More details by clicking 🌦️ or ⭐ icons in the table.

orientaciones.todos_title: Orientation filters are active. Click to clear all.

btn_info.tooltipVientoLimites:
Minimum, ideal, and maximum mean wind:

The automated condition scoring system will take these values into account.

The color of the data in the table visually indicates the condition analysis based on the values you set here (Min, Ideal, and Max).

🟧 Orange: light wind below your Minimum wind (usually 0, unless you require specific wind for ridge soaring or other reasons).
🟩 Green: Ideal wind, from minimum up to a safety margin before the Maximum.
🟧 Orange: stronger wind approaching the Maximum (the last third before reaching the Maximum).
🟥 Red: very strong wind exceeding your Maximum wind limit.

WMO (World Meteorological Organization) standard technical definition of 10m Mean Speed:

It is the average sustained wind speed over the last 10 minutes before the indicated time. It is measured at a standard height of 10 meters above the ground (avoiding immediate terrain friction). It represents the laminar or base flow.
btn_info.filtroDistanciaCompleto:
Takeoff distance filter:

Shows only takeoffs within the distance radius you select.

Use the button to set the origin position. You can use the map or your device's current location.

Use the button + to include non-favorite takeoffs in the filter, meaning to temporarily include all takeoffs available in the database.

When activated, an initial 100 km filter is applied to avoid overloading the table. You can adjust it based on your needs and device performance.
btn_info.tooltipRachaMaxima:
Maximum gust:

The automated condition scoring system will take these values into account.

The color of the data in the table visually indicates the condition analysis based on the value you set here.

🟩 Green: safe maximum gust, far from the limit.
🟧 Orange: maximum gust approaching your Maximum gust limit.
🟥 Red: gust exceeding your Maximum gust limit.

WMO (World Meteorological Organization) standard technical definition of 10m Maximum Gust:

It is the maximum peak of instantaneous speed (with a duration of 3 seconds) recorded at any time within the previous hour period. It represents the brief and intense fluctuations superimposed on the mean wind, associated with mechanical or thermal turbulence (convection).
btn_info.tooltipProbPrecipitacion:
Precipitation Probability (%)

Probability (%) that precipitation exceeds 0.1 mm (liters/m²).

Original data from the ECMWF model.
btn_info.tooltipVientoAlturas:
Wind at 80, 120 and 180 m (km/h)

Displays 3 rows with mean wind speed forecasts at 80, 120, and 180 m above ground level. These 3 rows are shown above the standard 10m mean wind speed row.

These values allow for evaluating the degree of shear turbulence and the reliability of the mean wind forecast.

Original data from the Arome/Arpege model (Météo-France).
btn_info.tooltipCizalladura:
Wind Shear / Reliability

Displays a thin row below the orientation row showing a calculated value: the Low-Level Wind Shear (LLWS) level and the degree of Reliability of the 10m mean wind forecast.

The system analyzes mean wind values at 10, 80, 120, and 180 m above ground level and, through an algorithm, shows a color-coded indicator.

🟩 Green: Low wind shear by speed and high reliability of the 10m speed forecast.
🟧 Orange: Moderate shear and reliability values.
🟥 Red: High wind shear by speed and low reliability of the 10m speed forecast.

This shear occurs when there is a high velocity gradient near the ground. This level is also correlative to the reliability of the wind speed at 10m above ground level.

The reason is that if the air mass moves at similar speeds, the degree of velocity-induced shear turbulence will be low, and the ground-level wind forecast will be more reliable, and vice versa.
btn_info.tooltipXC:
XC: Ceiling MSL, CAPE, CIN

Displays a group of 3 rows with data focused on Cross Country (XC) flight:
  • MSL Flight Ceiling (km): Maximum Boundary Layer Height (BLH)
  • CAPE (J/kg): Convective Available Potential Energy
  • CIN (J/kg): Convective INhibition
Original data from the ECMWF model.
btn_info.tooltipHorarioVuelo:
Preferred flying schedule:

The values configured here will cause these start and end times to be automatically selected when choosing a day of the week (Monday, Tuesday, etc.) in the time range selector located above the table.

You can always adjust the time range selector manually; this setting is just an automation to facilitate the automatic selection of a typical time range.

Whether done manually or automatically, only the final selected time range is taken into account for the Conditions score calculation; thus, each takeoff's score will be more realistic for that specific range.

If the Daylight hours only option is enabled, the range automatically selected with this setting will be adjusted to stay within daylight limits.
btn_info.tooltipHorasDiurnas:
Daylight hours only

Shows only daylight hours. If disabled, 24 hours of the day are shown.

A ±1h margin is included at sunrise and sunset.

Changes automatically every month. Additionally, in months with time changes, these hours will automatically adjust to the official time change (DST).
btn_info.tooltipOrdenarXC:
Sort by XC Conditions:

By default, takeoffs are sorted from best to worst based on the Takeoff Conditions score (feasibility of taking off according to the wind).

If you enable this option, the table will be sorted from best to worst XC Conditions score (thermal potential of the air mass), regardless of whether the wind at that takeoff is ideal or not.
btn_info.tooltipModelosMeteo:
Weather models:
  • Météo-France Arome-HD 1.3km: for wind data in the first 0-48h. Covers almost all of Europe, including the Iberian Peninsula, except for southern Andalusia (Arpege Europe 7km 0-96h is used in that area).
  • Météo-France Arpege Europe 7km: for wind data in the next 48-96h. Covers Europe and North Africa.
  • ECMWF IFS HRES 9km: for the rest of the weather data from 0-96h. Covers the entire world.
Automatic update frequency: Météo-France approximately every 3 hours (8 times a day) and ECMWF approximately every 6 hours (4 times a day).

Weather data gateway: Open-Meteo

The Ref.HHZ value indicates the UTC±0 (Z) time corresponding to the last execution cycle of the model on the source weather server. From that moment, there are several hours of computational calculation before final distribution and public availability.
